title: The Last One

jokes.text: box.. of... crap...
jokes.setup: Monica needs every box labeled properly for the move
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's brutally honest and unhelpful labeling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her oblivious honesty undermines Monica's perfectionism in the most efficient way possible

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, these boxes need to be labeled properly. I need to know what's in each one.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, okay! I got it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This one says 'Stuff.' This one says 'Other Stuff.' And this one is 'Things That Used to Be Alive.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe!

jokes.text: Wait. Should we be doing this? - I don't know. - It's quite not really a good idea. - Probably not.
jokes.setup: Two characters questioning their actions
jokes.punchline: Their increasing uncertainty while continuing to do whatever it is
jokes.explanation: Solid escalating doubt structure but nothing particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, why are we doing this?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I have no idea.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Should we stop?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Probably.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Are we going to stop?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, not really.

jokes.text: I love that our baby is prompt!
jokes.setup: Baby is coming three weeks early
jokes.punchline: Monica treating punctuality as a virtue even in premature birth
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Monica - finding a way to appreciate control and organization even in an uncontrollable situation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Three weeks early? That is so typical. I specifically asked this baby to arrive on schedule. I even gave it a written timeline.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you know how hard it is to plan a nursery around an unreliable birth date? I had everything color-coordinated for week 39!

jokes.text: Did you see the size of the thing that was coming out of that woman?
jokes.setup: Chandler witnessing childbirth
jokes.punchline: His horror at the reality of birth described crudely
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler squeamishness but fairly predictable birth humor

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm never having sex again.

jokes.text: I couldn't tell who is giving birth to who!
jokes.setup: Following up on the size comment
jokes.punchline: Absurd reversal of who's giving birth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from previous joke with absurdist twist

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not worried about the baby's size. I'm worried about Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, let's just say if this baby is as big as I think it's going to be, you're going to need a C-section. Actually, you're going to need a C-section, a D-section, and possibly an E-section.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying, I may end up giving birth to you.

jokes.text: I think that kid was wearing a shirt!
jokes.setup: Continuing the large baby theme
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about the baby being so large it's dressed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation cap with perfect absurdist detail - economical and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The baby's so big, they had to special order clothes from a tent manufacturer.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, this guy at work gave me 'Sex for Dummies' as a joke.
jokes.setup: Rachel commenting on Ross's improved moves
jokes.punchline: Ross's source was meant as mockery
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/payoff with good irony about the 'joke' gift actually working

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wow, Ross, your dancing has really improved!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Thanks! I've been watching this instructional video.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Who's it by?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler made it. He titled it 'How Not to Dance: A Guide by Ross Geller.'

jokes.text: Who's laughing now?
jokes.setup: The Sex for Dummies book was given as a joke
jokes.punchline: Ross's triumphant follow-up
jokes.explanation: Good escalation capping the joke sequence effectively

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I've read it cover to cover, and I'm here to tell you — it works!

jokes.text: No, last time you said it like Dracula, and it scared her!
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting to coach the breathing
jokes.punchline: His previous attempt was hilariously inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Chandler's inappropriate humor at the worst possible time

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't coach my breathing during labor!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? I'm great at this. Remember when I helped you practice?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was different.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How? I told you to breathe in... breathe out... in... out...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You told me to 'think of a happy place' and then described Hooters.

jokes.text: You can't leave me alone with her. - What? - This is exactly the kind of social situation that I am not comfortable with!
jokes.setup: Chandler being asked to stay with Erica
jokes.punchline: His panic at basic social interaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler social anxiety perfectly integrated into the high-stakes birth situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Stay with her? What do you mean stay with her? Like, in the same room?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, Chandler. Just for a little while. She's nervous.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nervous? Monica, I can barely talk to people I *know*. What am I supposed to say to her? 'Hey Erica, nice uterus'?

jokes.text: What kind of social situation are you comfortable with?
jokes.setup: Chandler's social discomfort
jokes.punchline: Monica's pointed observation about his general awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good character-based comeback that lands the observation about Chandler's universal awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you can't just stand there staring at people. You have to actually talk to them.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I am talking to them. I'm using my words.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Making weird noises is not talking, Chandler. And stop doing that thing with your face.

jokes.text: You'll be fine. Nah, you won't, but I'll be back in two minutes.
jokes.setup: Reassuring Chandler he'll be fine
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Monica's blend of supportiveness and brutal honesty in one economical line

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're going to be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, not at all. You're going to die alone.

jokes.text: So,... Any plans for the summer?
jokes.setup: Chandler alone with pregnant Erica in labor
jokes.punchline: His desperately inappropriate small talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - his social panic leading to hilariously inappropriate timing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, Erica, how are you feeling? You know, I gotta say, you're handling this really well. I mean, I've seen women in labor before—well, not before, but I imagine this is... this is going great. You're doing great. Could this BE any more of a contraction?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm just gonna sit here and not say anything. Because clearly, anything I say is going to be wrong. So I'm just gonna... [long pause] ...have you ever noticed how they never explain how they got off the island on Gilligan's Island?

jokes.text: I don't know. Maybe church-camp?
jokes.setup: Chandler's awkward summer plans question
jokes.punchline: Erica's innocent response highlighting the situation's absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - innocent response that makes Chandler's question even more inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what are you doing this summer?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Well, I'm 8 months pregnant, so I'll probably just stay home, lie on the couch, and try not to think about the fact that I'm carrying your babies.

jokes.text: Hah. May not wanna mention this.
jokes.setup: Erica mentioning church camp while in this situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awareness of the irony
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to the irony of the unwed pregnant teen mentioning church

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Church camp? Really? Because I gotta say, the irony of that is not lost on me.

jokes.text: So, you ever wonder which is worse, you know; going through labor or getting kicked in the nuts?
jokes.setup: Chandler desperately trying to make conversation
jokes.punchline: His spectacularly inappropriate topic choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler making the worst possible conversation choice in a delicate moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So... how about that decomposition? I read that after you die, your body breaks down and the gases make you swell up like a balloon. Could be a fun party trick!

jokes.text: One of life's great, unanswerable questions.
jokes.setup: His inappropriate labor/nuts question
jokes.punchline: Treating it as profound philosophical inquiry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation turning crude comparison into mock-philosophical statement

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could the peanuts be any more of a labor issue?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, think about it. The working conditions, the exploitation... it's a real philosophical question.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow, that's actually really deep, Chandler.

jokes.text: Like this..
jokes.setup: Chandler wondering about more painful things than labor or getting kicked in the nuts
jokes.punchline: Erica's implied reaction (likely a labor contraction) proving the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - Erica's pain immediately answers Chandler's philosophical question

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What could be more painful than labor or getting kicked in the nuts?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: *has a labor contraction*

jokes.text: It's a baby chick and duck! - Uh-huh. And I named them Chick Jr. and Duck Jr. - I did not see that coming.
jokes.setup: Joey's house-warming present reveal
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic response to Joey's predictable naming
jokes.explanation: Good callback with mild sarcasm about Joey's lack of creativity in naming

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I named it 'The Shining.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You named your apartment after a Stephen King novel?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, after the movie.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's based on a Stephen King novel.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I didn't know that.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Of course you didn't.

jokes.text: Yeah. It's a shame people can't visit there. - That is the rule, though.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the farm where the original chick and duck went
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's deadpan acknowledgment of the 'no visiting' rule at the fictional farm
jokes.explanation: Dark callback acknowledging the euphemistic 'farm' while maintaining the fiction for Joey's sake

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: They have a strict no-visiting policy.

jokes.text: Phoebe, you were sure Ben was gonna be a girl. - Have you seen him throw a ball?
jokes.setup: Ross challenging Phoebe's gender prediction accuracy
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's stereotypical justification for her original prediction
jokes.explanation: Relies on gender stereotypes but delivered with Phoebe's characteristic confidence

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you said it was going to be a boy. The ultrasound clearly showed it's a girl.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, but the baby was carrying low.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm not one to kiss and tell... but I'm also not one to have sex and shut up. We totally did it!
jokes.setup: Ross appearing to be discreet about what happened
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself and oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy - sets up discretion then immediately explodes it

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look, what happened between me and Rachel is private, okay? I'm not going to discuss it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We did it on the kitchen counter, the couch, and then again in the shower. It was incredible.

jokes.text: Oh, I feel like I'm in a musical!
jokes.setup: All the good news happening at once
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's theatrical reaction
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe whimsy that sets up the next joke

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! Oh my God! This is the best day of my entire life! I'm so happy I could just... I could just explode into a million pieces of pure joy right now!

jokes.text: Daa - raa... When the sun comes up, bright and beaming! And the moon comes...
jokes.setup: Phoebe feeling like she's in a musical
jokes.punchline: Her improvised terrible musical number
jokes.explanation: Sets up the interruption joke that follows

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I think I'm in a musical! ♪ There's a man... with a cat... and the cat's name is... Mittens! ♪ He's so... cat-like... and Mittens is... so... mit-tensy! ♪ Should we get married? Should we get divorced? These are the questions that plague us all! ♪

jokes.text: Morning! - Guess we'll never know how it ends.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's musical number being interrupted
jokes.punchline: Joey's mock-disappointment at the interruption
jokes.explanation: Good timing and deadpan delivery of mock disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Aw, come on! I was really enjoying that! Phoebe's got pipes, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, she was literally just humming.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Exactly! And you ruined it!

jokes.text: Chick and the duck? Didn't they die... - Dive!
jokes.setup: Rachel about to reveal the truth about the original pets
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's quick save with rhyming word substitution
jokes.explanation: Quick thinking wordplay to maintain the fiction

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, I have to tell you something about Mr. Whiskers and Fluffy...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! You mean Bizarro Mr. Whiskers and Bizarro Fluffy!

jokes.text: Yeah, they dove head-first into fun on the farm.
jokes.setup: Covering for the 'dive' mistake
jokes.punchline: Elaborate continuation of the farm fiction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that maintains the sweet lie while being absurd enough to be funny

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I grew up on a farm, and we had to be very creative with our food sources. My grandmother taught me how to make a delicious stew from whatever we could find in the fields.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, what kind of farm?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, it wasn't really a traditional farm. More like a... free-range situation. We'd just let things grow and see what happened. One year we got seventeen pounds of a vegetable we couldn't identify.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Seventeen pounds? What did you do with it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We made soup. It was either the best soup we ever had or the worst mistake of our lives. Either way, everyone was really quiet the next day.

jokes.text: it was just the perfect way to say goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ross thinking they're getting back together after their night
jokes.punchline: Rachel revealing it was just closure
jokes.explanation: Devastating reversal that sets up major plot tension

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So... last night was amazing. I've been thinking about us getting back together.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, last night was closure.

jokes.text: Ross's stunned silence
jokes.setup: Rachel's goodbye comment
jokes.punchline: Ross's shocked non-response
jokes.explanation: Effective silence beat showing Ross's devastation

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Goodbye, Ross.

jokes.text: I think it's time to kick you in the nuts and see which is worse!
jokes.setup: Erica in labor pain
jokes.punchline: Calling back to Chandler's earlier inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup - Erica using Chandler's own question against him

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Oh God, oh God, this hurts so much!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Remember when I asked if it was going to hurt?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wasn't asking for me.

jokes.text: Alright. Wow, that is one disgusting miracle.
jokes.setup: Monica calling birth 'the miracle of life'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest assessment after looking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - undercutting the moment with honest revulsion while acknowledging its significance

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's the miracle of life.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'd call it a miracle if I never have to see it again.

jokes.text: And some other stuff I'm gonna pretend I don't see.
jokes.setup: Monica celebrating the baby's birth
jokes.punchline: Her acknowledgment of the messy reality she's ignoring
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment from Monica maintaining optimism while acknowledging reality

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I have a beautiful baby. My life is perfect. Everything is clean and organized.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Who am I kidding? There's spit-up on my shoulder, I haven't showered in three days, and I'm pretty sure that's a diaper explosion on the couch.

jokes.text: Well, that's spongy.
jokes.setup: Chandler cutting the umbilical cord
jokes.punchline: His understated reaction to the texture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler understatement in a profound moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could this BE any more slimy?

jokes.text: Oh, I'm gonna love you so much that... no woman is ever gonna be good enough for you!
jokes.setup: Monica's emotional moment with her new son
jokes.punchline: Her immediate overprotective mother instincts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - her controlling nature instantly manifesting as a new mother

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's so perfect. I'm never letting anything bad happen to him. Ever.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Mon, he's been alive for like twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't care. This is my son. I will protect him from everything. The world is dangerous, Chandler. There are germs, and mean people, and—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And you're going to keep him in a bubble.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: If I have to!

jokes.text: I'm sorry, who should be along in a what now?
jokes.setup: Doctor mentioning the other baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confused panic
jokes.explanation: Good panic reaction showing Chandler's processing delay

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Congratulations, you're having twins!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Twins? Two babies? That's... that's great. Wait, whose is the other one?

jokes.text: We only ordered one!
jokes.setup: Learning about twins
jokes.punchline: Treating babies like a restaurant order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler panic response with great metaphor choice

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more excited about twins? I'm gonna need a bigger boat. Wait, wrong metaphor. I'm gonna need a bigger apartment. Actually, I'm gonna need a bigger EVERYTHING. Do you know what twins means? Two babies. Two! That's like ordering from a restaurant and saying, 'I'll have the baby, and I'll have another baby.' And they just BRING them both out.

jokes.text: These are the faces of two people in the know!
jokes.setup: Doctor's surprise they didn't know about twins
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response to their obvious ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to their obvious cluelessness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, did you not notice the two babies? I thought the matching outfits were a dead giveaway.

jokes.text: Well, gosh. That makes me feel so special and good.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying this has never happened before
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response to being a medical anomaly
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm in response to bad news

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: This has never happened before.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a little turned on right now.

jokes.text: Ah-ha... Join me, won't you?!
jokes.setup: Monica telling him not to panic
jokes.punchline: Chandler inviting her to join his panic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Chandler's characteristic dramatic flair

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Don't panic.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Too late. I'm already panicking. You wanna join?

jokes.text: Okay, what do you say we keep one, and then just like have an option on the other one?
jokes.setup: Panic about having twins
jokes.punchline: Treating babies like a business deal
jokes.explanation: Solid panic response with business metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Treating babies like a business deal? We're gonna need a better ROI strategy and a five-year plan!

jokes.text: We could give each of them half a medallion, and then years later, they'll find each other
jokes.setup: Monica asking why they can't split twins up
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate fairy tale reunion scenario
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation with specific fairy tale trope reference showing Chandler's imagination under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because somewhere down the line, they're gonna meet on a runway in Paris, and they're gonna lock eyes across a croissant, and know that they're connected by blood.

jokes.text: and be reunited I mean, that's a great day for everybody.
jokes.setup: The medallion reunion scenario
jokes.punchline: Chandler selling the emotional payoff
jokes.explanation: Good capping of the absurd scenario

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more emotionally unavailable? I'm selling the emotional payoff of this entire medallion reunion to the highest bidder.

jokes.text: What if it's adopted by a king? - Yeah, because I hear the king is looking to adopt.
jokes.setup: Chandler's optimistic adoption scenario
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic reality check
jokes.explanation: Good escalation/deflation pattern with Monica's practical sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So we'll adopt a baby, and everything will be perfect. We'll have a beautiful nursery, and the baby will sleep through the night, and we'll never argue about anything.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then we'll win the lottery, and I'll become a supermodel, and you'll finally get that acting job.

jokes.text: I don't care if it's three babies! I don't care if the entire cast of 'Eight is Enough' comes out of there!
jokes.setup: Monica declaring they'll take whatever babies they get
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to classic large family TV show
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect pop culture reference that fits the baby theme

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We'll take whatever babies we get!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Even if there's 20 of them?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Even if there's 20 of them!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Even if they're all horrible?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Even if they're all horrible! We are going to have a big, beautiful family!

jokes.text: Well, now we have one of each! And that's enough!
jokes.setup: Learning they have a boy and a girl
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately establishing limits after her previous unlimited declaration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's need to control reasserting itself immediately

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, but we're not doing any of that crazy stuff we said before.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No helicopter parenting, no participation trophies, no screen time before age five.

jokes.text: You can't just give up! Is that what a dinosaur would do? - What? - Dude, I'm just trying to speak your language.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to motivate Ross
jokes.punchline: His bizarre attempt at targeted motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his well-meaning but absurd attempt to connect with Ross through his interests

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Listen, Ross, you gotta get back out there. You're a paleontologist, right? So go be the best dinosaur guy you can be!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, I appreciate the sentiment, but that doesn't really help.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no, I'm not done. I'm visualizing it for you. You're in the museum, the dinosaurs are looking at you like 'That's our guy,' and you're just killing it with the fossils and the... the bones and stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, but—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? You're gonna go in there tomorrow and you're gonna tell those dinosaurs that Joey Tribbiani believes in you!

jokes.text: Oh, okay! This is the part of the musical where there'd be a really good convincing song.
jokes.setup: The group trying to convince Ross to pursue Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's meta-awareness of the musical structure
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor with Phoebe recognizing narrative structure

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You have to tell her how you feel! This is like, the second act breakup moment. If you don't do something now, we're never getting to the third act resolution!

jokes.text: Can't a girl finish a song around here?
jokes.setup: Getting interrupted again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's mock frustration at never getting to finish songs
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier musical interruption

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! Every time I start singing, someone interrupts me! I was just getting to the good part of 'Sticky Shoes.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Do you know how long I've been working on that song? Years! And nobody ever hears the ending because someone always—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. I give up. I'm never finishing another song again.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? Eight hours with my mother talking about Atkins? Good luck, Emma!
jokes.setup: Discussion of eight-hour flight with one-year-old
jokes.punchline: Rachel deciding her mother is the bigger challenge
jokes.explanation: Good reversal with relatable mother humor

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: An eight-hour flight with a one-year-old? That's nothing. You know what's really hard? Dealing with my mother.

jokes.text: I love you too. Probably not in the same way,...
jokes.setup: Gunther's love confession
jokes.punchline: Rachel's gentle but honest response
jokes.explanation: Perfect balance of kindness and honesty in an awkward moment

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Rachel, I've been wanting to tell you something for a long time. I'm in love with you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gunther... that's really sweet, but I don't feel the same way about you. I care about you, but only as a friend.

jokes.text: and when I'm in a café, having coffee, or I see a man with hair brighter than the sun, I'll think of you.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to let Gunther down easy
jokes.punchline: Her gentle reference to his distinctive blonde hair
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that acknowledges Gunther's most distinctive feature kindly

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gunther, you're such a sweet guy, and I really appreciate how much you care about me. But I just don't see us together romantically.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Is it because of my hair?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, no... I mean, it's very... golden.

jokes.text: Hey, you know what might help? - I'm not getting you a muffin!
jokes.setup: Joey about to make another request after Gunther's rejection
jokes.punchline: Ross anticipating Joey's muffin request
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ross learning Joey's patterns

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Could you just... could you get me a muffin?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, he just said no.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but maybe if you ask him...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No! No muffin!

jokes.text: Unless they're like two people who have lived in apartments next to each other for years, and then one day, they're pushed through a vagina and they meet.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether twins recognize each other
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate neighbor metaphor for twin birth
jokes.explanation: Creative and absurd analogy that's both specific and unexpected

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's like you're born next door to someone, and you spend nine months pressed against the wall listening to them, and then you're suddenly separated and you don't even know what they look like. But you recognize their cry from across a crowded room.

jokes.text: Oh my God, that's just like my name! - Son of a gun, it is!
jokes.setup: Telling Erica they're naming the baby after her
jokes.punchline: Her surprised realization and Chandler's mock amazement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Erica's innocence and Chandler's sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: You're naming the baby after me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, we wanted to honor the woman who made this all possible.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we figured it was the least we could do.

jokes.text: Even Chandler.
jokes.setup: Erica saying they'll make great parents
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded inclusion of Chandler
jokes.explanation: Nice understated burn that plays on everyone's awareness of Chandler's limitations

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: You guys are gonna make great parents. And Chandler, you'll be there too.

jokes.text: How sweet! Oh, is that the baby? - No, I sat in the paint.
jokes.setup: Monica seeing something that looks like a baby on Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with matter-of-fact delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? Oh, this? It's my baby.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your baby?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm babysitting for my friend. Well, actually, she doesn't know I have him yet.

jokes.text: How can you compare yourself to Gunther? I mean, sure, he's sexy in a more obvious way.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to encourage Ross by contrasting him with Gunther
jokes.punchline: Accidentally complimenting Gunther instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - trying to help but accidentally making it worse

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, man! You're way better than Gunther. You've got a real job, you're smart, you're confident...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Thanks, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, Gunther, he just stands there all day, makes coffee, pines after Rachel like some lovesick puppy... At least you have some dignity and self-respect.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you're doing it with real grace, man. Real grace.

jokes.text: It's... It's a city of Gunthers!
jokes.setup: Warning Ross about hot guys in Paris
jokes.punchline: Using Gunther as the standard for attractive men
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that creates a memorable phrase while building on the Gunther attractiveness theme

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, but just be careful, because Parisian men are very seductive.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I think I can handle it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, can you? Have you ever been compared to Gunther?

jokes.text: You know, the baby can't read, Mike!
jokes.setup: Mike's elaborate welcome sign for the baby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical observation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sweet gesture with practical reality

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's beautiful, Mike, but where's the baby gonna sleep?

jokes.text: Yeah, did she sound happy about it? 'Cause my friend Ethel's baby... was born with a teeny, tiny beard.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Monica's surprising news
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarre anecdotal reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe non-sequitur with specific bizarre detail

jokes.text: Now, what, what kinds are they? This is a boy, and that's a girl. - Her name is Erica. - Hey, that pregnant girl's name was Erica! - Yeah. It's a shame you two didn't get to spend more time together.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's innocent observation about the name coincidence
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic comment about the extensive time they spent together
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to Phoebe's obliviousness about the birth situation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, your name is Chandler and you're a chandelier maker!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not a chandelier maker.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, the universe is just funny sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, hilarious. I've only spent the last six years correcting people about this.

jokes.text: Jack Bing, Morning Gazette: 'I'm gonna blow this story wide open!'
jokes.setup: Discussion of the name Jack Bing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific 1940s reporter character impression
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with specific period reference and typical Chandler performance

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The name is Bing. Jack Bing. *adopts 1940s noir reporter voice* I'm a private eye, see? The dames come and go, but the bourbon stays cold.

jokes.text: Well, tell me which one, and I'll try slip it in my coat. Seriously.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she wants a baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler offering to steal one, then doubling down
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation with the 'seriously' adding commitment to the absurd offer

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can steal one for you.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm serious. I know a guy.

jokes.text: Ooh, we could teach them to sing, and we can be like the Von Trapp family! Only without the Nazis. Although that sounds kinda dull.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to make babies
jokes.punchline: Her Sound of Music reference ending with preferring the Nazi conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - sweet reference that takes a wonderfully dark turn that only she would make

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want to have babies. I want to have maybe four or five babies.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know exactly what I'm going to do. I'm going to move to the country, and I'm going to breed.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm gonna have sixteen babies.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, like Maria von Trapp in The Sound of Music. She had sixteen children.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But I'm not going to marry some guy and escape on a motorcycle. I'm just going to have the babies.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just sorry I'm not gonna be around to watch you two attempt to handle this!
jokes.setup: Rachel's goodbye to Monica and Chandler with twins
jokes.punchline: Her honest assessment of their parenting prospects
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment that acknowledges the challenge they're facing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You guys are gonna be great parents. I mean, look at you two — you're already arguing about whose turn it is to change the diaper, and the babies aren't even here yet.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Seriously though, I give it three weeks before Monica has a color-coded spreadsheet for their sleep schedules, and Chandler will have made approximately four thousand jokes about it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But honestly? They're lucky. Because no matter how insane you both are, those kids are gonna know they're loved.

jokes.text: they're gonna really hate me over there.
jokes.setup: Rachel's terrible French pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Her self-aware prediction about French reception
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about her linguistic limitations

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to France and I'm going to speak French.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And when I get there, they're going to take one look at me and go, 'Oh my God, she's American. She's going to butcher our beautiful language.'

jokes.text: They slept together? I was with you!
jokes.setup: Phoebe learning Ross and Rachel slept together
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused timeline protest
jokes.explanation: Good Joey confusion moment about timeline logistics

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You slept with Rachel? When?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Last night.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Last night?! But... but you were with us at the bar until like midnight!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How is that even possible?

jokes.text: Why there's an ass-shaped paint stain on my couch?
jokes.setup: Monica's happiness about everything working out
jokes.punchline: Noticing Phoebe's earlier paint incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings Monica back to her controlling nature even in a happy moment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe everything actually worked out perfectly!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... about that...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I may have gotten some paint on your couch earlier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: WHAT?!

jokes.text: Just your question, Mike!
jokes.setup: Monica's question about the paint stain
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dismissive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Mild deflection humor but not particularly strong

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that? I have no idea. Could be anything. Could be blood. Could be paint. Could be a painting of blood. I'm not a scientist.

jokes.text: That's okay. If we hit anything, the engine will explode, so you know, it's better if you're thrown from the car.
jokes.setup: No seatbelt in the cab
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's darkly logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Great dark Phoebe logic that makes the situation worse while trying to be reassuring

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, if we crash, I don't want to be trapped in the wreckage.

jokes.text: No, you don't understand. This isn't a real cab. - Alright, I gotta report you. What's your medallion number? - My medallion number is: Get out of the cab!
jokes.setup: Ross threatening to report the fake cab
jokes.punchline: Driver's medallion number being a direct threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected response format

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm going to report you to the taxi commission!

dialogue.speaker: Cab Driver
dialogue.line: Go ahead. My medallion number is 'You're Dead.'

jokes.text: Without looking under the diapers, can you tell them apart? - Ofcourse I can, I'm their mother! - Ok, which one is that one? - That's Jack.
jokes.setup: Chandler testing Monica's maternal instincts
jokes.punchline: Setup for the reveal that she's wrong
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the punchline that follows

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, I have something to tell you. I adopted a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Congratulations! When do I get to meet him?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's the thing. I need you to watch him while I go to work.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Of course! I'd love to! What's his name?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Rubber Ducky.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You named your baby Rubber Ducky?

jokes.text: Jack's gonna have a tough time in high school with that vagina!
jokes.setup: Monica confidently identifying 'Jack'
jokes.punchline: Chandler revealing she's looking at the girl
jokes.explanation: Strong punchline that deflates Monica's confidence with crude but effective delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's Jack.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's a girl.

jokes.text: Is the old woman on the bicycle still alive? - Yes, she jumped right back up.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about safety after Phoebe's driving
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of hitting an elderly cyclist
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's driving dangers with matter-of-fact delivery

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Pheebs, how many times have you hit something while driving?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, um... well there was that old man on the bike.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You hit an elderly man on a bike?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, he shouldn't have been in my way!

jokes.text: Damn, that window is clean.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to get money from glove compartment
jokes.punchline: Implication he hit the window instead
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy inference with understated reaction

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I need to get some money from the glove compartment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What happened to your hand?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The lock was stuck.

jokes.text: Oh, great! Just what you want for a new house with infants. Bird feces.
jokes.setup: Joey's chick and duck gift reveal
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical assessment of the gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler practical sarcasm about Joey's well-meaning but problematic gift

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, check it out! I got a chick and a duck!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A chick and a duck? Joey, what are you going to do with a chick and a duck?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: They're cute! And they're gonna live here with me!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great. And when they get big and the smell gets worse, that'll still be great.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I didn't know to look for Chinese food on the floor!
jokes.setup: Stepping on Phoebe's egg roll
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive response about floor food
jokes.explanation: Solid defensive response to an absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I didn't step on it, it fell on my foot. There's a difference.

jokes.text: Oh, tons, I'm quite the woodsman.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Chandler knows birdcalls
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic claim to outdoor expertise
jokes.explanation: Good character sarcasm about his obvious lack of outdoor skills

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know birdcalls?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, I'm very outdoorsy. I've spent a lot of time in nature. In fact, last weekend I was in Central Park for like, twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Twenty minutes?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I bought a hot dog.

jokes.text: It's like I would have said in that sci-fi movie, if I'd gotten the part. 'Those are our men in there, we have to get them out! Even if I have to sacrifice the most important thing in my life... my time-machine.'
jokes.setup: Joey preparing to break the foosball table
jokes.punchline: His elaborate reference to a role he didn't get with specific dialogue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his dramatic nature and acting aspirations coming through even in crisis

jokes.text: Did that movie ever get made? - It did not.
jokes.setup: Joey's elaborate movie reference
jokes.punchline: Revealing the movie never existed
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Joey's dramatic moment with practical reality

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's like that scene in 'Unbearable Lightness of Being'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not a real movie.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes it is!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, it's not. You just made that up.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I did not! It's a famous film!

jokes.text: Not since my cop-show got cancelled.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Ross has chased anyone through airports
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to having a cancelled cop show
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe mysterious past reference delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Have you ever chased anyone through an airport?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I had a show that got cancelled after two episodes.

jokes.text: because that's my bra-size.
jokes.setup: Rachel remembering her seat number
jokes.punchline: Her overly personal mnemonic device
jokes.explanation: Good oversharing moment with logical but inappropriate memory device

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My seat number is 12B. I remember it because it's my bra size and my birthday month.

jokes.text: Okay, fine! But you know what? If I was in 36D, we would not be having this problem.
jokes.setup: Security issues with her bra size seat number
jokes.punchline: Escalating the bra size reference
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the bra size theme but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe they're using my bra size as a security question. That's so invasive!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, at least it's something only you would know.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh really? Because apparently it's also my seat number on the airplane.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's... that's a lot of seats.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It gets worse. They're also using it for my locker combination at the gym.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Can I get that number?

jokes.text: I'm so lucky I married you.
jokes.setup: Pretending to be on honeymoon to get tickets
jokes.punchline: Phoebe selling the act to Ross
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation of their fake relationship cover story

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We're on our honeymoon.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You're on your honeymoon? With who?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: With my husband.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I didn't know you were married.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, we eloped. It was very romantic. We went to Vegas, got married by an Elvis impersonator.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's... that's not real.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's real to me, Ross.

jokes.text: Hah! I found it! I told you I would find it! In your face! - You're a different person.
jokes.setup: Rachel's triumphant discovery of her boarding pass
jokes.punchline: Agent's confused observation about her addressing the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good escalation deflation with confused authority figure

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I found it! I found my boarding pass!

dialogue.speaker: Gate Agent
dialogue.line: Ma'am, you've been talking to the water fountain for the last five minutes.

jokes.text: It's flight 421. Leaves at 8:40. - Yes, that's what I have. It's not on the board. - That's what it says here. Flight 421, leaves at 8:40, Newark airport. - What? - Newark airport. Why, where are you? - JFK.
jokes.setup: Ross not finding Rachel's flight
jokes.punchline: The devastating revelation of being at the wrong airport
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing and devastating reality check

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't find Rachel's flight!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What do you mean you can't find it?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I've checked everywhere - it's not here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross... which airport are you at?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: LaGuardia.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She's flying out of Newark.

jokes.text: And we're also gonna buy you tiny, bird hearing-aids.
jokes.setup: Promising to get the birds out
jokes.punchline: Joey's additional promise for their damaged hearing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific absurd detail

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll get the birds out, and I'll buy them hearing aids!

jokes.text: Table, you have given us so many great times. And you guys, Jordan, Victor, Joel... All of you guys, What can I say? You guys... make us... look good.
jokes.setup: Chandler's goodbye to the foosball table
jokes.punchline: His elaborate eulogy treating the foosball men as individuals
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with specific names and genuine emotion mixed with absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We gather here today to honor the memory of these brave foosball men. They've given us countless hours of joy, laughter, and competitive rage. Little Rod in the middle—we all knew him. Quiet. Dependable. He asked for nothing but a gentle spin and maybe some WD-40. The goalie... the unsung hero. How many times did he throw himself in front of a spinning ball for us? And these strikers on the sides—they lived fast, died young. They lived their entire lives in a three-inch arc.

jokes.text: Good game, good game, good game... good game.
jokes.setup: Joey's turn to say goodbye to the foosball table
jokes.punchline: His sports-style handshake goodbye to each player
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment treating the foosball players like real athletes with post-game courtesy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey guys, I just want to say goodbye to this table properly. You've been a real friend to me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Red team, you were solid. White team, you had your moments.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And to all of you out there on the field... it's been an honor.

jokes.text: I have this feeling that something's wrong with it. ... Something is wrong with the left phalange.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to get Rachel off the plane
jokes.punchline: Her made-up plane part with medical-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment creating a fake plane part using her knowledge of body parts (finger bones)

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! You can't get on that plane!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have a feeling something bad is going to happen on that plane.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Phoebe, that's crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No! I'm telling you, I have a premonition. The левian core is gonna fail!

jokes.text: Well... - Wait, what are you doing? - Well, I can't take this plane now.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's phalange warning
jokes.punchline: Another passenger getting scared and leaving
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phoebe's lie spreading

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, I have to say, for a blind woman, I'm having a great time on this plane.

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: Wait, you're blind?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: *gets up and leaves*

jokes.text: There is no phalange! - Oh my God. This plane doesn't even have a phalange! - I'm not flying on it!
jokes.setup: Flight attendant trying to correct the misinformation
jokes.punchline: Passenger interpreting the correction as confirmation of danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where the truth makes the situation worse - brilliant misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: Sir, that's not correct. We're not going down.

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we're not going down? Then where are we going?

jokes.text: Yeah, they were just slowing me down.
jokes.setup: Admiration for Phoebe not using tools to break the table
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact assessment of tools as hindrance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's unexpected practical side

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Tools are a crutch. They distance you from the work.

jokes.text: I guess I better get used to things crapping in my hand, huh?
jokes.setup: Joey holding the baby birds
jokes.punchline: His practical observation about his future with birds
jokes.explanation: Solid practical humor about pet ownership realities

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh no, the baby birds fell out of the nest. I gotta put them back.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... how do I know these are even my kids?

jokes.text: I could get a goose!
jokes.setup: Getting to keep the birds in his own room
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate plan to expand his poultry collection
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey escalation wanting more of a good thing

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, check it out! I'm gonna get a couple of ducks, maybe a goose. Oh! And a chicken!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, no.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? It'll be like a farm!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A farm? Joey, you can barely take care of yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm gonna call it 'Joey's Farm.' It's gonna be huge!

jokes.text: You know, I think you're set with the poultry.
jokes.setup: Joey wanting to get a goose
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical assessment of Joey's bird situation
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler understatement providing reality check

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm going to get a goose.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A goose? Joey, where are you going to keep a goose?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, I'll figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh sure, because that's worked out so well for you in the past.

jokes.text: Yes, the phalange is fixed. As a matter of fact, we put a whole lot of extra phalanges onboard, just in case.
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure passengers about the fake plane part
jokes.punchline: Going along with the fiction to calm passengers
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with flight attendant playing along with the absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: Ladies and gentlemen, I know you're all concerned about that part we found in the cockpit, but I want to assure you that it's probably not important. In fact, I'm sure the plane is better off without it. So just sit back, relax, and try not to think about which part of the plane might fall off next.

jokes.text: This is it. Unless we're on a break.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel reuniting and declaring no more messing around
jokes.punchline: Ross's reference to their infamous 'break'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the series' most famous relationship moment in their final reunion

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not messing around anymore. We're done with the games.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I agree. No more of this on-and-off thing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right. And just to be clear... we are NOT on a break.

jokes.text: Don't make jokes now.
jokes.setup: Ross's break joke
jokes.punchline: Rachel's serious response to his timing
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that grounds the moment after the callback

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We were on a break!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, we were together for three years. You don't just get to use that one line forever.

jokes.text: .. But more important.. .. because of rent control, it was a friggin' steal!
jokes.setup: Monica's sentimental goodbye to the apartment
jokes.punchline: Ending with the practical financial benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - undermining her own sentimentality with practical concerns in the finale

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This apartment meant everything to me. It was where I grew up, where I fell in love, where I built my life with Chandler and the kids.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But you know what? I'm going to miss the rent control even more.

jokes.text: Do you realise we almost made it ten years without that coming up?
jokes.setup: Phoebe bringing up Ross's dancing past
jokes.punchline: Ross's meta acknowledgment of avoiding embarrassment for the show's run
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke acknowledging the show's timeline and Ross's successfully hidden embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Remember when you used to dance?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh come on! You were always doing those moves!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That was a long time ago, and I would appreciate it if we never spoke of it again.

jokes.text: Where?
jokes.setup: The group deciding to get coffee
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical question about where to go now that Central Perk era is over
jokes.explanation: Perfect finale moment - one word that encapsulates the end of an era and their central gathering place

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So where are we gonna get coffee now that Central Perk is closed?

tmdb_overview:
Erica goes into labor and the new parents, Monica and Chandler, get a bit more than they bargained for. Meanwhile, Joey gets them a housewarming gift - Chick Jr. and Duck Jr., who unfortunately get trapped inside the foosball table. Also, after their night together, Ross realizes that he's still in love with Rachel and doesn't want her to leave. Other friends convince him to go after her and Phoebe races Ross to the airport in a bid to stop Rachel from leaving for Paris.

The six friends prepare to say goodbye and embark on the next chapters of their lives.
insight: Series finale prioritizes sentiment over laughs: 2.41 jokes/min, lowest density of Season 10.
